# Backyard Tomato Garden — Portland, OR

## Overview

This project documents a backyard vegetable garden at 3847 SE Division Street, Portland, OR 97202. The garden is managed by Sarah and Kevin Liu, who started growing tomatoes in 2024 and are expanding to a dedicated 400 sq ft growing area in 2026.

## Key Details

- **USDA Hardiness Zone**: 8b (minimum temps 15-20F)
- **Growing area**: 400 sq ft in four raised beds (4x8 ft each = 128 sq ft beds, plus two 4x12 ft beds = 96 sq ft each)
- **Soil type**: Willamette Valley clay loam, amended with compost
- **Sun exposure**: Full sun (8+ hours), south-facing backyard
- **Water source**: Municipal water from Portland Water Bureau, plus rain barrel collection (two 55-gallon barrels)
- **Season**: Last frost April 15, first frost October 25 (approximately 190-day growing season)

## Goals for 2026

The Lius want to grow enough tomatoes to supply fresh eating from July through September, plus can 40 quarts of tomato sauce and 20 pints of salsa for winter. Based on last year's yields, they estimate needing 24 tomato plants across four varieties. They are also experimenting with succession planting cherry tomatoes for continuous harvest.

## Garden Layout

- **Bed 1 (4x12)**: San Marzano paste tomatoes (8 plants, 18-inch spacing)
- **Bed 2 (4x12)**: Cherokee Purple heirloom (6 plants, 24-inch spacing)
- **Bed 3 (4x8)**: Sungold cherry tomatoes (4 plants, 18-inch spacing) + basil companion
- **Bed 4 (4x8)**: Early Girl slicers (6 plants, 18-inch spacing)

## Budget

Estimated total garden budget for 2026: $380 (seeds $25, soil amendments $120, drip irrigation $95, supports/cages $60, organic pest control $40, miscellaneous $40).
